candidates.snippet:
permissions:
  contents: read
  id-token: write
  pages: write

findings.title: OIDC (id-token: write) and pages: write granted workflow-wide, inherited by the build job that runs untrusted third-party code
findings.description: Permissions are declared at the workflow level — `id-token: write` and `pages: write` (L9-12) — so BOTH jobs inherit them, including the `build` job (L19-41). That build job runs a large amount of third-party/unpinned code under those privileges: `voidzero-dev/setup-vp@v1` (L24), `vp install` (arbitrary pnpm dependencies), `vp exec playwright install` and `vp run smoke` (L29-36). Only the `deploy` job (L43-51, `actions/deploy-pages@v4`) actually needs `id-token: write`/`pages: write`; the build job needs at most `contents: read`. With this over-grant, a single compromised build-time dependency or the unpinned setup action could mint OIDC tokens (usable against any cloud trust configured for this repo) and/or publish arbitrary content to the project's GitHub Pages site (defacement / malware under the project domain). The workflow only triggers on push-to-main and workflow_dispatch (write-access required), which bounds who can trigger it and keeps this at MEDIUM rather than HIGH.
findings.recommendation: Set workflow-level `permissions: { contents: read }` and grant `id-token: write` + `pages: write` only on the `deploy` job (job-level permissions). Keep the build job at read-only so compromised build dependencies cannot reach OIDC or Pages publishing.

findings.title: Actions pinned to mutable tags instead of commit SHAs
findings.description: All actions are referenced by mutable major tags: `actions/checkout@v4` (L22), third-party `voidzero-dev/setup-vp@v1` (L24), `actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3` (L39), `actions/deploy-pages@v4` (L51). Because this workflow holds `id-token: write` and `pages: write` (see the permissions finding), a re-pointed tag — most concerning for the third-party `voidzero-dev/setup-vp@v1` — would run attacker code with OIDC/Pages publishing capability. Pinning to immutable commit SHAs removes the moved-tag supply-chain vector.
findings.recommendation: Pin every action to a full commit SHA with a trailing version comment (e.g. `voidzero-dev/setup-vp@<sha> # v1`), and enable Dependabot for github-actions to keep the pins current.
